mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, directly increasing present valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate crossing above 5.0 percent signals a structural labor market deterioration that reduces household consumption capacity and compresses corporate earnings, driving negative equity returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in greater than 60 percent of triggered instances despite the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in real consumer spending and housing starts indicate a synchronized demand shock across durable and non-durable sectors, forcing immediate equity repricing downward.
failure_condition: Joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative returns with a Rank IC greater than 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth turning negative while inflation remains elevated creates a stagflationary supply shock that compresses profit margins and triggers negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO growth coincides with positive SPX returns in greater than 55 percent of cases when CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth exceeds 3 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month payroll employment growth directly tracks corporate revenue capacity and labor cost stability; positive payroll growth sustains earnings momentum and generates positive forward equity returns.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero predictive power for 20-day returns with a Rank IC below 0.01 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: The consumer price index crossing above 210.0 signals entrenched inflation expectations that prompt market pricing of aggressive future rate hikes, compressing equity valuations and driving negative returns.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL values above 210.0 coincide with positive SPX returns in greater than 65 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly real spending indicates data revision risk and weak underlying demand, causing institutional investors to reduce equity allocations and drive negative returns.
failure_condition: GDPC1 and RSAFS divergence fails to predict negative returns with a Rank IC greater than 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: The industrial production index level acts as a real-time proxy for aggregate economic output; higher index levels correlate with stronger corporate earnings and positive forward equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO level shows no statistical relationship with 20-day forward returns with a Rank IC below 0.01 across all tested windows.
